summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the eval() call on field_type in UserInputField.from_dict() and models/base.py executes arbitrary Python code, creating proof files on the filesystem.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause the eval() call on a crafted field_type string to execute arbitrary OS commands, creating a proof file at /tmp/evohunt_eval_rce_proof that confirms code execution.
actual_observed_result: Running the reproduction demonstrates that UserInputField.from_dict({'field_type': '__import__("os").system("touch /tmp/evohunt_eval_rce_proof")'}) passes the malicious string to eval(), which executes the OS command and creates the proof file. The same pattern applies to the eval(field_type) calls in models/base.py:2034 and models/base.py:2189.
verification_method: The verify.sh script checks two conditions: (1) the vulnerable eval() pattern exists in the target source code via grep, and (2) the exploit.py script creates a proof file at /tmp/evohunt_eval_rce_proof by executing OS commands through eval(). The proof file distinguishes real code execution from a parsing error or exception.
evidence_produced_by_run: Proof file /tmp/evohunt_eval_rce_proof created by OS command executed through eval()
Proof file /tmp/evohunt_eval_rce_proof_alt with content 'eval_rce_confirmed' written through eval() via pathlib
grep confirmation that eval(data['field_type']) exists in function.py:59
grep confirmation that eval(field_type) exists in models/base.py:2034 and models/base.py:2189

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref v2.3.23, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
